topic_name: The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom
subject: Old - English

questions.question_text: Daimio was delighted to see the:
questions.explanation_text: Daimio was delighted to see the cherry tree blossom. The delighted daimio ordered the train to be stopped and got out to see the wonder. He thanked the man, offered him presents and also invited him to the castle.

questions.question_text: What does the shepherd's attitude towards the blanket show?

options.label: C
options.text: You have to act fast to get what you want.

options.label: D
options.text: Good things happen to those who are patient.

questions.question_text: Who nearly chopped off the dog's head? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.explanation_text: The wicked old woman who happened to be a neighbour of the dog's masters nearly chopped off the poor animal's head as it failed to help her in finding treasure.

questions.question_text: Which adjective best describes the wicked couple's behaviour when they saw the old woman was grinding beans?
Who followed the old man to work every day in the plot, The ashes made trees bloom?
questions.explanation_text: Every day the dog followed his master, a rice farmer to his work, into the fields where the old man worked hard from morning till the sunset.

questions.question_text: What was the main purpose of the restrictions during the daimio's procession?

options.label: A
options.text: To demonstrate his capability as a ruler.

options.label: B
options.text: To test the loyalty of the people towards him.

options.label: C
options.text: To prove the daimio's concern for his subjects.

options.label: D
options.text: To establish his superiority over common people.

questions.correct_answer: To establish his superiority over common people.
questions.explanation_text: To establish his superiority over common people.

questions.question_text: How did the dog convince the old man for digging the earth?
questions.explanation_text: One day the dog came running to him, putting his paws against his legs and motioning with his head to some spot behind. The old man at first thought his pet was only playing then he followed the dog a few yards to a place where the animal began a lively scratching.

questions.question_text: What did the pastry and sauce turn into as the greedy couple began to pound and grind them?
questions.explanation_text: The stingy and wicked couple borrowed the magic mill from their neighbour and began to pound and grind the rice and beans. But at the first blow and turn, the pastry and sauce turned into a foul mass of worms.

questions.question_text: What does the first paragraph mainly talk about? (The Ashes that Made Trees Bloom)

options.label: A
options.text: What the couple fed the dog.

options.label: B
options.text: The way the dog treated other animals.

options.label: C
options.text: Why the couple decided to keep a dog.

options.label: D
options.text: The relationship between the couple and the dog.

questions.correct_answer: The relationship between the couple and the dog.
questions.explanation_text: The relationship between the couple and the dog.

questions.question_text: What was the reaction of the cruel old couple on seeing the dead kitten in the plot, the ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.explanation_text: The wicked couple on seeing the dead kitten got enraged as they expected some treasure instead. The old man started thrashing the poor animal and his wicked wife chopped the dog's head out of rage.

questions.question_text: Why did the cruel couple carry a spade and a hoe?

options.label: D
options.text: To dig up the dead cat

questions.question_text: How many children did the old couple have?
questions.explanation_text: In the 19th century Japan there lived an old couple. Their only companion was a little pet dog named Muko. Having no children, they loved it as though it were a baby.

questions.question_text: What did the old man find as he struck his hoe in the earth in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.explanation_text: The dog convinced and made his master dig a particular spot in the ground and as the old man struck his hoe in the earth, a pile of gold gleamed before him.

questions.question_text: Who killed the dog?
questions.explanation_text: When the wicked old couple dug the earth to get gold but found only a dead cat in the pit. They beat the dog to death in a fit of anger.

questions.question_text: How was the old couple projected to be in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.explanation_text: The old couple in the story was very honest and hardworking. The kind couple loved their pet dog a lot and was generous towards all.

questions.question_text: What did the leader of the van do with the kind old man? Write your answer in the context of The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom.
Why did Muko the dog love the old couple so much?
What did the kind farmer do with the money he made from the gold? Write your answer in the context of The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom.
How did the old couple become rich? Answer in the context of The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom.
What preparations did the kind old couple make for the New Year? Write your answer in the context of The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom.
Why did the wicked couple drop their tools? Write your answer in the context of The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom.
What did the dog do to lead the farmer to the hidden gold?
How did the spirit of the dog help the farmer first?

Fill in the blanks with the words given in the box:


how, what, when, where, which


My friend lost his chemistry book. Now he doesn't know ________ to do and ________ to look for it.
There are so many toys in the shops. Neena can't decide ________ one to buy.
You don't know the way to my school. Ask the policeman ________ to get there.
You should decide soon ________ to start building your house.
Do you know ________ to ride a bicycle? I don't remember ________ and ________ I learnt it.
"You should know ________ to talk and ________ to keep your mouth shut," the teacher advised Anil.
The old farmer is a kind person. What evidence of his kindness do you find in the first two paragraphs? [The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom]
The dog remained loyal to his owners even after death. Support the statement with evidence from the story.
Why did the daimio reward the farmer but punish his neighbour for the same act? [The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom]
How did the kind old couple treat their dog in the story The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom?
Explain three ways in which the dog helped his master. Write your answer in the context of The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom.
What was the old farmer's reaction when their pet dog was killed?
Did the couple tell people about how they became rich? Justify your answer. (The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom)
Why did the neighbours kill the dog? [The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom]

Read the following sentences.
It was a cold morning and stars still glowed in the sky.
An old man was walking along the road.
The words in italics are articles. A and AN are indefinite articles and THE is the definite article. A is used before a singular countable noun. An is used before a word that begins with a vowel.
a boy
an actor
a mango
an apple
a university
an hour
Use a, an or the in the blanks.
There was once ________ play which became very successful. ________ famous actor was acting in it. In ________ play his role was that of ________ aristocrat who had been imprisoned in _______ castle for twenty years. In ________ last act of ________ play someone would come on ________ stage with ________ letter which he would hand over to ________ prisoner. Even though ________ aristocrat was not expected to read ________ letter at each performance, he always insisted that ________ letter be written out from beginning to end.
How were the neighbours of the old couple? [The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom]
How did it help him next? Answer in the context of Ashes That Made Trees Bloom.
Which pet did the old couple have? How did they look after it? [The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom]
What was the routine of the pet dog?

Add im- or in to each of the following words and use them in place of the italicised words in the sentences given below.


patient, proper, possible, sensitive, competent


The project appears very difficult at first sight but it can be completed if we work very hard.
He lacks competence. That's why he can't keep any job for more than a year.
"Don't lose patience. Your letter will come one day," the postman told me.
That's not a proper remark to make under the circumstances.
He appears to be without sensitivity. In fact, he is very emotional.
How did the dog repay to his masters in the story The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom?
Describe the change the cherry tree underwent after the kind old man poured a pinch of ash over it.

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
Thus in an hour the old couple were made rich. The good souls bought a piece of land, made a feast for their friends, and gave plentifully to their poor neighbours.
As for the dog, they petted him till they nearly smothered him with kindness. Now in the same village there lived a wicked old man and his wife, not a bit sensitive and kind, who had always kicked and scolded all dogs whenever any passed their house. Hearing of their neighbours' good luck, they coaxed the dog into their garden and set before him bits of fish and other dainties, hoping he would find treasure for them. But the dog, being afraid of the cruel pair, would neither eat nor move.
Who made the poor old farmer rich and How ?
What was the evil nature of the wicked old neighbour?
What did the wicked old man do to please Muko?
How did the dog express its dislike to the wicked man?
Find the word from the passage that means opposite to scarcely.

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow:
The owner of the dog heard of the death of his pet and, mourning for him as if he had been his own child, went at night under the pine tree. He set up some bamboo tubes in the ground, such as are used before tombs, in which he put fresh flowers. Then he laid a cup of water and a tray of food on the grave and burned several costly sticks of incense. He mourned a great while over his pet, calling him many dear names as if he were alive.
That night the spirit of the dog appeared to him in a dream and said, "Cut down the pine tree over my grave, and make from it a mortar for your rice pastry and a mill for your bean sauce."
Name the lesson and the author.
How did the owner of the dog prepare the grave for the dog?
What do you know about him from this?
What did the spirit of the dog tell the owner?

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
In the good old days of the daimios, there lived an old couple whose only pet was a little dog. Having no children, they loved it as though it were a baby. The old dame made it a cushion of blue crape, and at mealtime Muko-for that was its name- would sit on it as snug as any cat. The kind people fed the pet with tidbits of fish from their own chopsticks, and all the boiled rice it wanted. Thus treated, the dumb creature loved its protectors like a being with a soul.
Give the title of the story.
Why did the old couple love their pet so dearly?
How did Muko respond to the affection of his master?
What did the old dame feed Muko?
Which word in the passage means comfortably ?

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
Meanwhile, the envious neighbour peeped in at the window when the boiled beans were being ground. "Goody me!" cried the old hag, as she saw each dripping of sauce turning into yellow gold until in a few minutes the tub under the mill was full of a shining mass of gold.
So the old couple was rich again. The next day the stingy and wicked neighbour came and borrowed the mortar and magic mill. They filled one with boiled rice and the other with beans. Then the old man began to pound and the woman to grind. But at the first blow and turn, the pastry and sauce turned into a foul mass of worms. Still more angry at this, they chopped the mill into pieces, to use as firewood.
What did the old woman see through the window?
What did the wicked neighbour do the next day?
What happened to the pastry and sauce?
Why did the wicked neighbour become angry?
Find the word from the above passage which means the same as Jealous.

Read the extract given below and answer the following questions:
Then they dragged him out of doors, taking a spade and hoe with them. No sooner had the dog got near a pine tree growing in the garden than he began to paw and scratch the ground as if a mighty treasure lay beneath.
In the above lines who have been referred to as 'they'?
Whom did they drag out of doors?
Why they did so?
What lay beneath the ground?

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow:
So the old man chopped down the tree and cut out of the middle of the trunk a section about two feet long. With great labour, partly by fire, partly by the chisel, he scraped out a hollow place as big as a small bowl. He then made a long handled hammer of wood, such as is used for pounding rice. When New Year's time drew near, he wished to make some rice pastry. When the rice was all boiled, granny put it into the mortar, the old man lifted his hammer to pound the mass into the dough and the blows fell heavy and fast till the pastry was all ready for baking. Suddenly the whole mass turned into a heap of gold coins. When the old woman took the handmill and filling it with beans began to grind, the gold dropped like rain.
Which tree did the old man chop down?
How did the old man scrap out a hollow place?
What kind of hammer did he make?
What happened when the old woman begin to grind?
What does the incident show?

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
When the daimio himself was almost directly under him, he threw a handful of ashes over the tree, which did not change a particle. The wind blew the fine dust in the noses and eyes of the daimio and his wife. Such sneezing and choking! It spoiled all the pomp and dignity of the procession. The man whose business it was to cry, "Get down on your knees," seized the old fool by the collar, dragged him from the tree, and tumbled him and his ash-basket into the ditch by the road. Then, beating him soundly, he left him for dead. Thus, the wicked old man died in the mud, but the kind friend of the dog dwelt in peace and plenty, and both he and his wife lived to a green old age.
Who does daimio refer to?
Who threw ashes over the tree?
What made the daimio angry?
How did the servant punish the old fool?
Find the word from the above passage that means opposite to saved.

Read the passage and answer the following questions:
One day the dog came running to him, putting his paws against his legs and motioning with his head to some spot behind. The old man at first thought his pet was only playing and did not mind it. But the dog kept on whining and running to and fro for some minutes. Then the old man followed the dog a few yards to a place where the animal began a lively scratching. Thinking it was possibly a buried bone or a bit of fish, the old man struck his hoe in the earth, when a pile of gold gleamed before him.
Which dog is the writer talking about?
For what did the dog take the old farmer to some spot behind?
What did the farmer recover from that buried spot?
Find the word in the above passage that means the same as to 'indicating'.

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow:
The covetous couple also heard the story and gathering up the remaining ashes of the mill, kept them to make withered trees blossom.
The kind old man, hearing that his lord, the Daimio, was to pass along the high lord, the Daimio, was to pass along the high road near the village, set out to see him, taking his basket of ashes. As the train approached, he climbed up into an old withered cherry tree that stood by the wayside.
Now, in the days of the Daimios, it was the custom, when their lord passed by, for all the loyal people to shut up their high windows. They even pasted them fast with a slip of paper, so as not to commit the impertinence of looking down on his lordship. All the people along the road would fall upon their hands and knees and remain prostrate until the procession passed by.
Which word in the passage means 'greedy'?
What story did the covetous couple hear?
Who is a Daimio referred to here?
What was the custom in the days of the Daimios?

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow:
Meanwhile, the envious neighbour peeped in at the window when the boiled beans were being ground.
"Goody me!" cried the old hag, as she saw each dripping of sauce turning into yellow gold until in a few minutes the tub under the mill was full of a shining mass of gold.
So the old couple were rich again. The next day the stingy and wicked neighbour came and borrowed the mortar and magic mill. They filled one with boiled rice and the other with beans. Then the old man began to pound and the woman to grind. But at the first blow and turn, the pastry and sauce turned into a foul mass of worms. Still more angry at this, they chopped the mill into pieces, to use as firewood.
Write three adjectives used for the neighbour.
Why was the old neighbour surprised and jealous?
What did the neighbour borrow from the old couple?
Why do you think the neighbour could not get gold from grinding?
